title: [Feature]: Evaluate a Solace sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists Solace under streaming and events. nats-sinks does not yet have a Solace sink or event-broker compatibility profile.
proposal: Research a Solace sink using supported Solace APIs or protocols. The design should compare direct Solace publishing, JMS compatibility, MQTT/AMQP options if relevant, persistent messaging, transactions or acknowledgements, duplicate handling, and routing metadata mapping.
users: Organizations using Solace event brokers that want NATS JetStream events forwarded into existing event meshes.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires persistent Solace acceptance for every required message. Best-effort or non-persistent publishing must not be the default for durable sink mode.
security: Broker URLs, credentials, VPN names, topic names, and payloads must be redacted. TLS verification and least-privilege publish permissions are required.
acceptance: Research identifies the preferred Solace integration API.
The design documents persistent delivery and duplicate behavior.
Configuration validates topics, endpoints, timeouts, and authentication mode.
Unit tests use fakes and no broker network calls.
Documentation explains relationship with generic JMS or AMQP-style connectors.
tests: Add fake client tests for publish confirmation, error translation, and ACK ordering if implemented. Gate live tests.
documentation: Add Solace sink evaluation documentation and roadmap entry.
closeout: Close after release of the research or implementation.
